title: String Methods
content:
### String Methods

JavaScript provides many **built-in methods** to manipulate strings.

---

### Common Methods

| Method | Description | Example | Result |
|--------|-------------|---------|--------|
| `length` | Returns character count | `"Hello".length` | `5` |
| `toUpperCase()` | Converts to uppercase | `"hello".toUpperCase()` | `"HELLO"` |
| `toLowerCase()` | Converts to lowercase | `"HELLO".toLowerCase()` | `"hello"` |
| `trim()` | Removes whitespace from both ends | `"  hi  ".trim()` | `"hi"` |
| `slice(start, end)` | Extracts a part | `"Hello".slice(0, 3)` | `"Hel"` |
| `substring(start, end)` | Similar to slice | `"Hello".substring(1, 4)` | `"ell"` |
| `replace(old, new)` | Replaces first match | `"a-b-c".replace("-", ":")` | `"a:b-c"` |
| `split(separator)` | Splits into array | `"a,b,c".split(",")` | `["a","b","c"]` |
| `includes(str)` | Checks if contains | `"Hello".includes("ell")` | `true` |
| `indexOf(str)` | Returns position | `"Hello".indexOf("e")` | `1` |

---

### Method Chaining

Methods can be **chained** together:

```js
let text = "  Hello World!  ";
let result = text.trim().toUpperCase().slice(0, 5);
console.log(result); // "HELLO"
```

---

> **Strings are immutable:** Methods return a **new** string, they do not change the original.

quiz.question: Which method converts a string to uppercase in JavaScript?
